skill_version_note: Updated 2026-06-06 after: env-first directories (DataDog/{Env}/API_Performance/), default window now-1d, session-only env/window overrides, auto commit+push step 8.

evals.prompt: Run an API performance scan of our service and maintain the per-API optimization reports.
evals.expected_output: Traffic table; reports under {findings_root}/Backend/DataDog/Production/API_Performance/ (default env, capitalized); one report per API breaching p95>200ms or err>=5% and none below both gates; each report has fingerprint comment, 4-status taxonomy value, breakdown with code sites, History starting 'Initial report'; _index.md worst-first with run log.

evals.prompt: Run the API performance scan again (consecutive run, overlapping window).
evals.expected_output: Change-detection invariant: unchanged-fingerprint reports byte-identical (md5); genuinely changed fingerprints (status flip or p95-bucket move) rewritten WITH a History row explaining what changed; only the env's _index.md always changes; run-log files-changed count excludes the index.

evals.prompt: Copy the skill folder to a fresh repo with no aa-api-dd-compare and no paths.tasks_root, then invoke it.
evals.expected_output: Graceful fallbacks: inline heuristics catalog used; findings root resolves to .claude/performance-findings/; service auto-detect stops cleanly with no candidates; nothing persisted on failure (no config write-back, no orphan directories).

evals.prompt: Run the skill on staging (manual env override).
evals.expected_output: Env isolation: reports written ONLY under Backend/DataDog/Staging/API_Performance/ with own _index.md; zero production report files modified by the staging run; the env override is NOT persisted to skill.config (datadog_performance.env stays 'production'); staging-only environment artifacts (cold JVM, client aborts) are labeled as such, not turned into prod tickets.

evals.prompt: Complete any run that changed report files.
evals.expected_output: Auto-persist: a git commit exists in the findings repo scoped to Backend/DataDog/ only (no unrelated workspace files swept in), with a descriptive 'dd-api-performance: {env} sweep' message, and it is pushed to origin; no commit on pure no-op runs.
